skillability_rationale: fzf is a highly focused, well-documented CLI tool with a clear task boundary: interactively filter and select from arbitrary text streams using fuzzy matching. It has a stable, predictable stdin/stdout interface, integrates seamlessly into pipelines (e.g., 'find . | fzf'), and requires no network or external services. Its deterministic local execution, minimal dependencies, and widespread adoption make it trivial to wrap as an agent-callable skill for search/filter tasks across files, processes, history, etc.
name: junegunn/fzf
description: :cherry_blossom: A command-line fuzzy finder

skillability_rationale: Mem0 provides a well-defined, task-oriented memory abstraction—storing, retrieving, and evolving semantic memories for AI agents. Its clean SDK/API (Python/JS), clear use cases (personalization, RAG augmentation, state management), and production-ready remote service make it highly skillifiable: it wraps a focused capability (long-term memory management) as an agent-callable unit with deterministic inputs/outputs, low integration overhead, and high automation ROI in agent workflows.
name: mem0ai/mem0
description: Universal memory layer for AI Agents

skillability_rationale: jq is a highly focused, battle-tested CLI tool for querying and transforming JSON data. Its interface is declarative, consistent, and composable via pipes; it runs deterministically with no side effects or external dependencies. It integrates seamlessly into agent workflows (e.g., parsing API responses, extracting config values) and can be invoked locally or in containers with minimal setup. Low operational risk due to immutability, zero network calls, and idempotent execution.
name: jqlang/jq
description: Command-line JSON processor

skillability_rationale: sharp provides a well-defined, atomic capability—high-performance image processing (resize, convert, rotate, composite, etc.)—with a clean, consistent, and well-documented JavaScript API. It runs deterministically locally, has minimal external dependencies on modern systems, integrates seamlessly into Node.js/Deno/Bun workflows, and exposes discrete operations that are easily wrapped as agent-callable functions (e.g., 'resize_image', 'convert_format', 'extract_region'). Its narrow scope, strong typing via options objects, and stream/Promise/callback support make it highly composable and low-risk to operationalize as a skill.
name: lovell/sharp
description: High performance Node.js image processing, the fastest module to resize JPEG, PNG, WebP, AVIF and TIFF images. Uses the libvips library.

skillability_rationale: OpenHands provides a well-defined, task-oriented agent framework for autonomous software development (e.g., issue resolution, code generation, testing, debugging), with clear CLI, SDK, and GUI interfaces. It wraps LLMs and tools into reproducible dev workflows — making it highly skillifiable as a workflow-level capability. Deployment friction arises from dependencies (Docker, LLM backends) and configuration, slightly lowering score. Not a primitive tool or service wrapper, but a composable, opinionated workflow skill for code-centric automation.
name: OpenHands/OpenHands
description: 🙌 OpenHands: AI-Driven Development

skillability_rationale: FastMCP is a production-ready, opinionated framework for building and consuming Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers and clients. It wraps MCP's protocol complexity into simple Python decorators and APIs, enabling developers to expose or invoke tools (e.g., 'add', 'search_db') as standardized, LLM-accessible capabilities. Its clear task boundary (exposing/invoking discrete tools via MCP), excellent interface (declarative @tool decorator, auto-generated schemas), high composability (tools integrate into larger agent workflows), and strong automation value (enables reliable, auditable tool calling for LLM agents) make it highly skillifiable — not as a primitive or workflow, but as a robust service wrapper for connecting agents to external capabilities.
name: PrefectHQ/fastmcp
description: 🚀 The fast, Pythonic way to build MCP servers and clients.

skillability_rationale: Tesseract.js provides a well-defined, self-contained OCR capability with clear input (image URL or blob) and output (structured text + confidence metrics). It exposes a simple, consistent async API (`createWorker`, `recognize`, `terminate`) that is easily wrapable as an agent skill. Runs locally in browser or Node.js without external dependencies or auth, minimizing operational risk. High automation value for document digitization tasks. Minor risk stems from variable OCR accuracy depending on image quality and language model availability, but this is inherent to OCR—not a design flaw.
name: naptha/tesseract.js
description: Pure Javascript OCR for more than 100 Languages 📖🎉🖥

skillability_rationale: Ultralytics YOLO provides a well-defined, task-oriented API for computer vision tasks (detection, segmentation, pose estimation, tracking) with CLI and Python interfaces. It wraps complex deep learning models into deterministic, callable units that accept images/videos and return structured predictions — ideal for agent integration. Minimal external dependencies beyond PyTorch/CUDA, clear input/output contracts, high automation value in real-world perception pipelines, and low operational risk (no external API keys or SaaS lock-in). Slight deployment friction arises from GPU/CUDA requirements, but containerized or CPU-fallback usage remains viable.
name: ultralytics/ultralytics
description: Ultralytics YOLO 🚀

skillability_rationale: Lux provides a well-defined, CLI-driven capability to download videos from dozens of supported platforms (YouTube, Bilibili, etc.) with clear inputs (URLs) and outputs (downloaded files). It wraps complex scraping/downloading logic into a deterministic, local executable — making it highly skillifiable as a service wrapper. Deployment requires Go or prebuilt binaries and optional FFmpeg, adding mild friction. Operational risk stems from anti-bot measures, rate limiting, and site-specific fragility, but its focused scope and mature CLI interface justify strong automation value and high skillability.
name: iawia002/lux
description: 👾 Fast and simple video download library and CLI tool written in Go

skillability_rationale: Day.js is a highly focused, deterministic, zero-dependency library for date/time parsing, formatting, and arithmetic — ideal as a reusable primitive tool. It has excellent API clarity, trivial deployment (no setup, no network calls), and composes seamlessly into larger workflows. While extremely valuable for time-related automation (e.g., generating timestamps, validating deadlines, computing durations), it doesn’t encapsulate an end-to-end user task on its own — it’s a foundational utility, not a workflow skill. Low operational risk due to immutability, wide browser/node support, and mature stability.
name: iamkun/dayjs
description: ⏰ Day.js 2kB immutable date-time library alternative to Moment.js with the same modern API

skillability_rationale: LocalAI provides a unified, OpenAI-compatible API wrapper for diverse generative tasks (text, image, audio, video, voice cloning) that can be cleanly exposed as discrete agent-callable skills. Its local-first, no-GPU-required design enables reliable deterministic execution; REST/gRPC interfaces are well-documented and standardized; models are swappable via config; and it avoids external dependencies or vendor lock-in. Deployment requires local resource provisioning but is otherwise self-contained and secure — making it highly skillifiable as a composable, high-value, low-risk multimodal inference service.
name: mudler/LocalAI
description: :robot: The free, Open Source alternative to OpenAI, Claude and others. Self-hosted and local-first. Drop-in replacement,  running on consumer-grade hardware. No GPU required. Runs gguf, transformers, diffusers and many more. Features: Generate Text, MCP, Audio, Video, Images, Voice Cloning, Distributed, P2P and decentralized inference

skillability_rationale: TA-Lib/ta-lib-python provides a well-defined, deterministic set of technical analysis functions (e.g., RSI, MACD, candlestick patterns) over time-series financial data — making it highly task-clear and composable as a primitive tool. Its Python API is stable, well-documented, and integrates cleanly with Pandas/Polars. Deployment friction arises from native C dependencies (requiring TA-Lib C library installation), lowering deployment score slightly. Operational risk is moderate due to numerical sensitivity and assumption of clean input data, but not inherently unsafe. It’s skillifiable because it encapsulates a bounded, reusable analytical capability that agents can invoke programmatically for decision-support in trading or analytics workflows.
name: TA-Lib/ta-lib-python
description: Python wrapper for TA-Lib (http://ta-lib.org/).

skillability_rationale: RSSHub provides a well-documented, RESTful API to fetch structured feeds (e.g., latest tweets, Weibo posts, YouTube videos) from hundreds of websites — a canonical data retrieval task. Its routes are predictable, deterministic, and require no authentication for most sources. It wraps otherwise non-RSS web content into standardized RSS/Atom feeds, making it highly composable in automation pipelines (e.g., feed → parser → notifier). While self-hosting adds minor deployment friction, public instances (rsshub.app) enable zero-deployment usage. Low operational risk: stateless, idempotent, no side effects.
name: DIYgod/RSSHub
description: 🧡 Everything is RSSible

skillability_rationale: SurrealDB exposes a well-defined, REST/gRPC/WebSocket API with SQL-like query language (SurrealQL) for CRUD, realtime subscriptions, and graph/document operations — making it highly suitable as an agent-callable data service. It’s not a primitive tool (e.g., CLI-only) nor a full platform adapter (it doesn’t orchestrate other systems), but a robust service wrapper. Deployment requires provisioning a server or cloud instance (moderate friction), and operational risk is moderate due to statefulness and consistency trade-offs in distributed mode. Its rich client SDKs and clear task boundaries (e.g., 'fetch user orders with live updates') support high composability and automation value.
name: surrealdb/surrealdb
description: A scalable, distributed, collaborative, document-graph database, for the realtime web

skillability_rationale: ProxyPool provides a well-defined, task-oriented service: fetching and validating usable HTTP/S proxies on demand via a simple REST API (e.g., GET /get). It wraps complex proxy harvesting, verification, and Redis-backed pooling logic into a consumable interface. While it requires Redis setup and config tuning (moderate deployment friction), its API is stable, documented, and composable into crawling workflows. Operational risk stems from reliance on volatile free proxy sources and potential IP blacklisting—but this is inherent to the domain, not poor design. It is not a primitive tool (it’s not a single function) nor a full platform; it’s a focused service wrapper for proxy acquisition.
name: jhao104/proxy_pool
description: Python ProxyPool for web spider

skillability_rationale: Standard is a well-defined, CLI-driven JavaScript linter and auto-formatter with zero-config defaults. It exposes clear, atomic tasks (lint, fix, check) via predictable command-line interface, integrates seamlessly into CI/CD or editor tooling, and delivers high automation value for code quality enforcement. Its deterministic local execution, low operational risk (no side effects beyond file modification), and widespread adoption make it highly skillifiable as a reusable agent capability for code standardization.
name: standard/standard
description: 🌟 JavaScript Style Guide, with linter & automatic code fixer

skillability_rationale: Turf provides a rich, well-documented set of deterministic geospatial operations (e.g., buffering, intersection, distance calculation) over GeoJSON inputs/outputs. Each function is atomic, pure, and composable; integrates easily into JS/TS agents via npm; has zero external dependencies or side effects; and poses minimal operational risk (no network calls, auth, or state). Its clear input/output contracts and widespread real-world usage make it highly skillifiable as a reusable primitive for location-aware agent tasks.
name: Turfjs/turf
description: A modular geospatial engine written in JavaScript and TypeScript

skillability_rationale: The Silver Searcher ('ag') is a highly focused, CLI-based code search tool with a well-defined input (search pattern + optional file/directory scope + flags) and deterministic output (matching lines with context). It exposes a stable, widely adopted interface, integrates cleanly into pipelines (e.g., via shell scripts or agent subprocess calls), has low operational risk (no network dependencies, no state, no side effects), and delivers high automation value for developer-facing search tasks. Its narrow, task-bound nature makes it ideal as a reusable primitive skill.
name: ggreer/the_silver_searcher
description: A code-searching tool similar to ack, but faster.

skillability_rationale: PostgREST exposes a well-defined, RESTful interface over PostgreSQL, enabling precise CRUD operations and filtered queries via HTTP. It wraps database access as a deterministic remote service with clear input (URL/query params) and output (JSON). While it requires deployment and DB configuration (moderate friction), its standardized API makes it highly composable into agent workflows for data retrieval, reporting, or integration tasks. Not a primitive tool (it's not code-level), nor a full platform — it's a focused, production-ready service wrapper. Operational risk is moderate due to dependency on DB state and auth setup, but low for read-heavy use cases.
name: PostgREST/postgrest
description: REST API for any Postgres database

skillability_rationale: puppeteer-extra wraps Puppeteer with a clean, plugin-based API to automate browser tasks (e.g., stealth browsing, reCAPTCHA solving, ad blocking) in a deterministic, locally executable way. Its modular design, strong documentation, and widespread adoption make it highly skillifiable: each plugin exposes a well-defined capability (e.g., 'solve_recaptcha', 'evade_detection') that can be invoked as an atomic agent action. Deployment requires Node.js and Chromium but is otherwise self-contained and scriptable.
name: berstend/puppeteer-extra
description: 💯  Teach puppeteer new tricks through plugins.

skillability_rationale: PDFMathTranslate provides a well-defined, high-value task — AI-powered, layout-preserving translation of scientific PDFs — with clear CLI, GUI, Docker, and MCP interfaces. It wraps multiple LLM/translation backends (OpenAI, DeepL, Ollama, etc.) into a unified, deterministic local execution model. Its purpose is narrow and repeatable, easily composable into research or localization workflows, and carries low operational risk (no persistent state or external account dependencies by default). Deployment friction is moderate due to optional dependencies (e.g., LaTeX for math rendering), but Docker and pip packaging significantly lower the barrier. It exemplifies a production-grade, skillifiable document processing capability.
name: PDFMathTranslate/PDFMathTranslate
description: [EMNLP 2025 Demo] PDF scientific paper translation with preserved formats - 基于 AI 完整保留排版的 PDF 文档全文双语翻译，支持 Google/DeepL/Ollama/OpenAI 等服务，提供 CLI/GUI/MCP/Docker/Zotero

skillability_rationale: Postgres.js is a highly focused, well-documented database client that enables precise SQL execution and result handling — ideal as a deterministic primitive tool for data retrieval, parameterized queries, and transactional operations. Its clean tagged-template API makes intent explicit and composable within agent workflows. Deployment friction arises from requiring environment configuration (credentials, network access) and runtime dependencies (Node.js/Deno), but no external service mediation or human input is needed. Not a full workflow skill or platform adapter — it’s a foundational, low-level data interaction tool.
name: porsager/postgres
description: Postgres.js - The Fastest full featured PostgreSQL client for Node.js, Deno, Bun and CloudFlare

skillability_rationale: GHunt provides well-defined, CLI- and library-accessible OSINT capabilities (e.g., email, Gaia ID, Drive file lookup) against Google services — making it highly task-bound and automatable. It wraps Google's undocumented/internal APIs with clear inputs/outputs (JSON export supported), enabling composition into larger workflows. Deployment requires Python 3.10+ and login setup (cookies via browser extension), adding mild friction. Operational risk is elevated due to reliance on reverse-engineered endpoints and AGPL licensing constraints, limiting enterprise embedding. Still, it’s a robust, deterministic, local service wrapper for targeted data retrieval — strongly skillifiable as an agent tool.
name: mxrch/GHunt
description: 🕵️‍♂️ Offensive Google framework.

skillability_rationale: SVGO provides a well-defined, atomic task—optimizing SVG files—with clear CLI and programmatic interfaces. It has no external dependencies or side effects, runs deterministically locally, and is trivially composable into larger workflows (e.g., build pipelines, CMS asset processing). Its configuration is explicit, safe (no destructive defaults), and widely adopted in production. Low operational risk: failures are non-catastrophic (output is invalid SVG or unchanged), and inputs/outputs are pure files.
name: svg/svgo
description: ⚙️ Node.js tool for optimizing SVG files

skillability_rationale: The repository exposes a well-defined, API-first interface for retrieving and downloading video content (no-watermark) from Douyin, TikTok, and Bilibili. It is clearly task-bound (parse + download), production-ready (FastAPI, async, deployed public API), and easily integrable as an agent-callable service. Risks include platform anti-bot measures, ToS ambiguity, and dependency on external signature/CDN logic — lowering operational robustness. Deployment requires minimal setup (Docker or pip), but self-hosting may need proxy/signature maintenance, hence deployment_friction=3.
name: Evil0ctal/Douyin_TikTok_Download_API
description: 🚀「Douyin_TikTok_Download_API」是一个开箱即用的高性能异步抖音、快手、TikTok、Bilibili数据爬取工具，支持API调用，在线批量解析及下载。

skillability_rationale: ESLint provides a well-defined, task-bounded capability—static analysis and auto-fixing of JavaScript code—which can be cleanly wrapped as an agent-callable tool (e.g., 'lint and fix this code snippet'). It has a stable CLI and programmatic API, strong interface clarity, and high automation value. Deployment friction arises from Node.js/runtime dependencies and config setup, slightly lowering accessibility. Low operational risk: failures are non-destructive and local. Not quite a 5 due to configurability complexity (custom rules, plugins, configs) requiring context-aware invocation.
name: eslint/eslint
description: Find and fix problems in your JavaScript code.

skillability_rationale: Nano ID provides a well-defined, atomic capability—generating cryptographically secure, URL-friendly unique IDs—with zero dependencies, minimal footprint, and deterministic local execution. It’s highly composable and trivial to integrate into any agent workflow requiring ID generation (e.g., creating resource identifiers, session tokens, or trace IDs). While extremely reliable and low-risk, its automation value is moderate because it solves a narrow, often already-solved problem; agents rarely need to *choose* or *configure* ID generation as a top-level task—it’s usually an internal implementation detail rather than a user-facing skill.
name: ai/nanoid
description: A tiny (118 bytes), secure, URL-friendly, unique string ID generator for JavaScript

skillability_rationale: FFmpeg provides highly clear, well-documented, task-oriented CLI tools (ffmpeg, ffprobe, ffplay) for multimedia conversion, analysis, and streaming — ideal for wrapping as agent-callable skills. Its deterministic local execution, rich input/output control, and composability via command-line flags make it strongly skillifiable. Deployment friction arises from binary dependencies and platform-specific compilation, but prebuilt binaries and containerization mitigate this. Risk is moderate due to potential codec licensing or malformed input crashes, but not severe in controlled environments.
name: FFmpeg/FFmpeg
description: Mirror of https://git.ffmpeg.org/ffmpeg.git

skillability_rationale: ffuf is a well-defined, CLI-driven web fuzzer with clear task boundaries (e.g., directory, vhost, parameter discovery) and deterministic local execution. It exposes structured input (wordlists, templates, filters) and produces parseable output (JSON, CSV), enabling reliable agent integration. Deployment requires Go or binary installation (minor friction), and composability is strong via stdin/stdout and flags—but it lacks native API or async hooks, limiting it to primitive-tool granularity. Operational risk is moderate due to its offensive security nature (potential for unintended server load or detection), requiring careful policy-aware orchestration.
name: ffuf/ffuf
description: Fast web fuzzer written in Go

skillability_rationale: Rollup is a well-defined, deterministic bundling tool with clear CLI and JS API interfaces. It performs a bounded, repeatable task (bundling ES modules) that integrates cleanly into build pipelines. While it requires configuration (e.g., input/output, format, plugins), its behavior is predictable and low-risk. Deployment friction arises from needing Node.js and proper config setup, but once configured, it’s highly automatable. Not a workflow or service wrapper — it’s a foundational primitive for JavaScript development automation.
name: rollup/rollup
description: Next-generation ES module bundler

skillability_rationale: The Notion SDK is a well-documented, officially supported TypeScript client that wraps the Notion REST API with strongly typed, intuitive method names (e.g., notion.pages.create, notion.databases.query). It has clear task boundaries (CRUD operations on Notion objects), requires only an auth token, and integrates cleanly into agent workflows via async/await. It’s not a framework or tutorial—it’s a production-ready service wrapper with high composability, low interface ambiguity, and direct automation value for knowledge management and collaboration tasks.
name: makenotion/notion-sdk-js
description: Official Notion JavaScript Client

skillability_rationale: CocoaPods provides a well-defined, CLI-driven dependency management capability for iOS/macOS Xcode projects (e.g., 'pod install', 'pod update'). It wraps complex Xcode project integration logic into deterministic, scriptable operations. While highly skillifiable as a devops automation tool, its maintenance-mode status and tight coupling to local Ruby/Xcode environments slightly increase operational risk and deployment friction—e.g., version compatibility, Ruby runtime, Xcode toolchain dependencies. Still, it’s easily callable via shell commands and composes well into CI/CD or agent workflows managing iOS Swift/Objective-C projects.
name: CocoaPods/CocoaPods
description: The Cocoa Dependency Manager.

skillability_rationale: The tool provides a well-defined, end-to-end task — converting e-books (EPUB, etc.) to structured audiobooks with chapter segmentation, metadata, voice cloning, and multilingual TTS. It exposes clear inputs (file + config) and outputs (audio files), has a stable CLI and Gradio GUI, supports programmatic invocation, and wraps complex TTS/voice-cloning pipelines into a single callable unit. Its Docker/Colab/HF Spaces deployments confirm production readiness for agent integration. Low abstraction leakage and high practical utility make it highly skillifiable.
name: DrewThomasson/ebook2audiobook
description: Generate audiobooks from e-books, voice cloning & 1158+ languages!

skillability_rationale: Bytebase provides a well-defined, task-oriented platform for database schema change management, CI/CD, and security governance — all accessible via REST API and Terraform. Its clear boundaries (e.g., 'create migration', 'approve change', 'detect drift') make it highly skillifiable as a remote API-mediated platform adapter. Deployment friction is moderate due to infrastructure requirements (DB, reverse proxy, auth setup), and operational risk is elevated only in production-critical contexts — but not prohibitive for agent orchestration of routine DevOps tasks.
name: bytebase/bytebase
description: World's most advanced database DevSecOps solution for Developer, Security, DBA and Platform Engineering teams. The GitHub/GitLab for database DevSecOps.

skillability_rationale: Dub provides well-defined, API-driven capabilities for link shortening, click attribution, UTM tracking, and affiliate link management — all high-value, discrete business operations tasks. Its REST API (documented at https://docs.dub.co) enables reliable remote invocation. While self-hosting adds complexity (moderate deployment friction), the cloud-hosted SaaS version offers low-friction integration. It composes well with marketing, analytics, and CRM workflows but is not a primitive tool or full platform — it wraps a focused service. Operational risk is low due to mature infrastructure, observability, and idempotent link creation/tracking semantics.
name: dubinc/dub
description: The modern link attribution platform. Loved by world-class marketing teams like Framer, Perplexity, Superhuman, Twilio, Buffer and more.

skillability_rationale: Nodemailer provides a well-defined, task-oriented interface for sending emails programmatically — a clear, high-value automation capability. It wraps SMTP/transport logic into a simple JS API, making it highly skillifiable as a reusable 'send email' action. Requires configuration (auth, transport) and error handling in production, adding moderate deployment friction and operational risk (e.g., credential management, deliverability issues), but remains deterministic and composable within agent workflows.
name: nodemailer/nodemailer
description: ✉️ Send e-mails with Node.JS – easy as cake!

skillability_rationale: cloudflared is a well-defined, CLI-driven tunneling service wrapper that exposes clear, task-oriented commands (e.g., 'tunnel create', 'tunnel run', 'access ssh'). It automates secure zero-trust connectivity to private origins without firewall changes — high automation value. While it requires Cloudflare account setup and domain configuration (moderate deployment friction), its interface is stable, documented, and composable via config files or CLI args. Operational risk stems from network-critical role and dependency on Cloudflare’s infrastructure, but it’s production-hardened. Not a primitive tool (it orchestrates tunnels) nor a full platform — hence 'service_wrapper'.
name: cloudflare/cloudflared
description: Cloudflare Tunnel client

skillability_rationale: Dasel provides a well-defined, atomic capability—querying, extracting, and mutating structured data across multiple formats via a consistent CLI interface. It has clear input/output semantics, no external dependencies or state, and integrates cleanly into pipelines. Its Go library mode also enables embedding as a deterministic, side-effect-free function. Low operational risk: it operates on immutable inputs (unless explicitly writing in-place) and fails fast on invalid queries or formats.
name: TomWright/dasel
description: Select, put and delete data from JSON, TOML, YAML, XML, INI, HCL and CSV files with a single tool. Also available as a go mod.

skillability_rationale: remarkable is a focused, well-documented Markdown parser with a clear input (Markdown string) → output (HTML string) contract. It exposes a simple, synchronous API, runs locally without external dependencies or side effects, and is widely used in production (e.g., Docusaurus). Its deterministic behavior, rich option surface, and plugin extensibility make it highly composable and low-risk to wrap as an agent-callable skill for tasks like rendering, sanitizing, or transforming Markdown content.
name: jonschlinkert/remarkable
description: Markdown parser, done right. Commonmark support, extensions, syntax plugins, high speed - all in one. Gulp and metalsmith plugins available. Used by Facebook, Docusaurus and many others! Use https://github.com/breakdance/breakdance for HTML-to-markdown conversion. Use https://github.com/jonschlinkert/markdown-toc to generate a table of contents.

skillability_rationale: acme.sh provides a well-defined, task-bound capability—automating ACME-based SSL/TLS certificate issuance, renewal, and installation—via simple CLI commands (e.g., 'acme.sh --issue', '--renew', '--install-cert'). It wraps external CA APIs (Let's Encrypt, ZeroSSL, etc.) into a portable shell interface, making it highly automatable and composable in infrastructure workflows. Interface is CLI-driven with clear flags and exit codes; however, shell dependency, privilege requirements (e.g., sudo for webroot or cert installation), and environment-specific quirks (e.g., DNS plugin setup) introduce moderate deployment friction and operational risk. Not a primitive tool (it orchestrates multi-step protocols) nor a full platform—hence 'service wrapper'. Its deterministic local execution and high automation value make it strongly skillifiable.
name: acmesh-official/acme.sh
description: A pure Unix shell script ACME client for SSL / TLS certificate automation

skillability_rationale: The tool provides a well-defined, CLI-based capability to download and decrypt HLS/m3u8 streams into playable media files — a clear, bounded multimedia task. It exposes rich, documented command-line options, making it highly interface-clear and automatable. As a .NET Framework Windows binary, deployment requires runtime installation (moderate friction) and lacks cross-platform support, limiting composability in heterogeneous environments. Operational risk is moderate due to reliance on external keys/headers and potential DRM/legal constraints (e.g., unsupported platforms like Youku), but core AES-128-CBC decryption and merging are deterministic. It’s easily wrapable as an agent-executed service (e.g., via subprocess), scoring high on task clarity, automation value, and skillability.
name: nilaoda/N_m3u8DL-CLI
description: [.NET] m3u8 downloader 开源的命令行m3u8/HLS/dash下载器，支持普通AES-128-CBC解密，多线程，自定义请求头等. 支持简体中文,繁体中文和英文. English Supported.

skillability_rationale: Mypy is a well-defined, task-oriented static type checker with a clear CLI and programmatic API. It can be reliably invoked on Python code to detect type errors — a high-value, repeatable devops task. While it requires local Python environment setup (moderate deployment friction), its deterministic behavior, excellent documentation, and integration-friendly design (e.g., exit codes, JSON output mode) make it highly skillifiable as a primitive tool for automated code quality gates.
name: python/mypy
description: Optional static typing for Python

skillability_rationale: AnythingLLM is a self-hosted, all-in-one RAG + agent platform that enables users to chat with documents, run AI agents, and manage knowledge workflows locally. Its core capability—ingesting, indexing, and querying private documents via LLMs—is well-scoped, repeatable, and task-oriented. It exposes REST APIs and supports MCP (Model Context Protocol), enabling integration as a workflow-level skill. Deployment requires Docker or Node.js setup (moderate friction), but once running, it operates deterministically offline with low operational risk (no external API dependencies or rate limits). Interface clarity is high for end-users (UI/UX) and decent for developers (API docs exist but are not SDK-first). Composability is strong via MCP, webhooks, and plugin hooks—though not as granular as a primitive tool.
name: Mintplex-Labs/anything-llm
description: The all-in-one AI productivity accelerator. On device and privacy first with no annoying setup or configration.

skillability_rationale: GitDiagram provides a well-defined, high-value task—generating interactive system architecture diagrams from GitHub repos via AI—and exposes it through a clean public API (WIP but functional). It wraps OpenAI-powered analysis and Mermaid rendering into a single callable capability. While self-hosting requires backend setup (moderate deployment friction), the core function is deterministic, composable (e.g., feed repo URL → get diagram/Mermaid/PNG), and low-risk for read-only public repos. Private repo support adds auth complexity but doesn’t undermine skillability. Not a primitive tool (it’s opinionated and end-to-end) nor a full platform—hence 'service_wrapper'.
name: ahmedkhaleel2004/gitdiagram
description: Free, simple, fast interactive diagrams for any GitHub repository

skillability_rationale: Maxun provides a well-defined, no-code workflow abstraction (robots) for web data tasks—scraping, crawling, extraction, search—with clear inputs (URLs, selectors, natural language prompts) and structured outputs (JSON, Markdown, screenshots). It wraps Playwright-based browser automation into reusable, shareable, and schedulable units. The platform exposes an SDK and API, enabling integration into agent systems as callable skills. Task boundaries are explicit, interfaces are intuitive (recorder + AI mode), and it delivers high automation value with moderate operational risk (e.g., anti-bot detection). Self-hosting lowers dependency risk, and its design aligns natively with agent skill invocation patterns.
name: getmaxun/maxun
description: 🔥 The open-source no-code platform for web scraping, crawling, search and AI data extraction • Turn websites into structured APIs in minutes 🔥

skillability_rationale: Recast provides a well-defined, deterministic, and widely adopted capability: parse-modify-print JavaScript ASTs with source map preservation. Its API is minimal, stable, and composable — ideal for agent skills that perform code transformation (e.g., refactoring, codemodding, lint-fixing). It runs locally, requires no external services or human input, and has low operational risk (no side effects, pure functions). Deployment friction is low (npm install + import), though TypeScript/JS toolchain awareness is assumed.
name: benjamn/recast
description: JavaScript syntax tree transformer, nondestructive pretty-printer, and automatic source map generator

skillability_rationale: golangci-lint is a well-defined, CLI-based Go linter runner with clear input (Go source files/directories) and output (lint reports). It’s highly automatable in CI/CD or editor integrations, has stable CLI interface, and minimal side effects. Deployment friction arises from Go runtime dependency and platform-specific binaries, but Docker and prebuilt binaries mitigate this. Its composability is strong via config files and exit codes, though it’s not inherently modular—it’s a single-purpose tool. Low operational risk: it’s read-only, non-destructive, and widely trusted in production Go workflows.
name: golangci/golangci-lint
description: Fast linters runner for Go

skillability_rationale: vCluster provides a well-defined, CLI-driven capability to create and manage isolated virtual Kubernetes clusters within a host cluster — a clear infrastructure provisioning task. Its interface (vcluster CLI + Helm) is mature and well-documented. It composes well with CI/CD and GitOps tooling but requires Kubernetes access and cluster-level permissions, raising operational risk and deployment friction (e.g., RBAC setup, resource quotas). Not a primitive tool nor a full workflow, but a platform adapter bridging physical clusters to tenant-scoped control planes.
name: loft-sh/vcluster
description: vCluster - Create fully functional virtual Kubernetes clusters - Each vcluster runs inside a namespace of the underlying k8s cluster. It's cheaper than creating separate full-blown clusters and it offers better multi-tenancy and isolation than regular namespaces.

skillability_rationale: Guardrails provides a well-defined, task-oriented capability—validating and structuring LLM inputs/outputs—via declarative validators and guard definitions. It wraps LLM calls with deterministic, composable checks (e.g., 'valid-url', 'no-sql-injection'), exposing a clean Python API. While deployment requires integrating into an LLM pipeline (moderate friction), it’s highly automatable, low-risk (fails safely, no side effects), and designed for reuse across workflows. Not a primitive tool (it orchestrates validation logic) nor a full platform—hence 'service_wrapper'.
name: guardrails-ai/guardrails
description: Adding guardrails to large language models.

skillability_rationale: commitlint is a well-defined, task-specific devops tool that validates commit message structure against conventional standards. It has a clear CLI interface, deterministic local execution, low operational risk (read-only validation), high automation value in CI/CD and pre-commit hooks, and integrates cleanly into agent workflows as a lightweight, composable linter — making it highly skillifiable as a primitive devops utility.
name: conventional-changelog/commitlint
description: 📓 Lint commit messages

skillability_rationale: Ibis provides a clear, high-level, SQL- and dataframe-oriented interface for querying and transforming data across dozens of backends. It wraps heterogeneous database and dataframe systems into a unified, deterministic, callable API — ideal for agent-driven data retrieval, filtering, aggregation, and export tasks. Its lazy evaluation, backend-agnostic expressions, and single-line backend switching make it highly composable and automation-friendly with low operational risk (no side effects, no stateful services required). Deployment friction is moderate only due to optional backend dependencies (e.g., DuckDB, Snowflake drivers), but core functionality is lightweight and embeddable.
name: ibis-project/ibis
description: the portable Python dataframe library

skillability_rationale: idb provides well-defined, CLI-driven primitives for iOS simulator/device automation (e.g., install, launch, record, log tail), making it highly task-clear and composable in CI/CD or test orchestration. It runs locally but requires macOS + companion daemon setup (moderate deployment friction). Operational risk is moderate due to reliance on private Apple frameworks and iOS version sensitivity, but it’s battle-tested at scale. Not a full workflow skill — it’s a low-level, deterministic infrastructure tool ideal for agent-mediated iOS automation.
name: facebook/idb
description: idb is a flexible command line interface for automating iOS simulators and devices

skillability_rationale: goose provides a well-defined, CLI- and library-accessible interface for executing database schema migrations — a clear, bounded infrastructure task. It supports multiple databases and migration types (SQL/Go), making it composable in CI/CD or provisioning workflows. Deployment friction is moderate due to driver dependencies and DB connection setup, and operational risk is elevated (migrations are irreversible and can corrupt state), requiring safeguards. Still, it’s highly skillifiable as an agent-executable infrastructure operation with deterministic outcomes when inputs (driver, DSN, migration dir, command) are fully specified.
name: pressly/goose
description: A database migration tool. Supports SQL migrations and Go functions.

skillability_rationale: ast-grep provides a well-defined, CLI-based structural code search, linting, and rewriting capability with clear input (patterns, files, rules) and deterministic output (matches, diffs, rewritten code). It is lightweight, language-agnostic (via Tree-sitter), scriptable, and integrates cleanly into CI/CD or agent workflows. No network dependency or human judgment required for core tasks; low operational risk. Its pattern-matching semantics are precise and composable via config files or chained invocations.
name: ast-grep/ast-grep
description: ⚡A CLI tool for code structural search, lint and rewriting. Written in Rust

skillability_rationale: Lerna provides a well-defined, CLI-driven workflow for monorepo management—versioning, publishing, and dependency linking—making it highly suitable as an agent-callable skill. Its interface is stable and documented, though requires local Node.js/npm setup and repo context (moderate deployment friction). It composes well with CI/CD pipelines and other devtooling. Risk is moderate due to potential side effects on package versions and published artifacts, but mitigated by dry-run flags and conventional commits. Not a primitive tool nor platform adapter—it orchestrates a coherent devops task.
name: lerna/lerna
description: Lerna is a fast, modern build system for managing and publishing multiple JavaScript/TypeScript packages from the same repository.

skillability_rationale: OpenSearch is a production-grade search engine with well-defined REST APIs for indexing, querying, aggregating, and managing data — making it highly task- and interface-clear. It’s composable via API calls (e.g., embedded in agent workflows for semantic search or log analytics), but requires deployment/management of a cluster or connection to a managed instance, introducing moderate deployment friction and operational risk (e.g., scaling, security, uptime). As a remote-service-backed capability—not a library or CLI tool—it fits cleanly as a service wrapper skill. Not a primitive_tool (too heavy) nor platform_adapter (no ecosystem bridging), and doesn’t require human-in-the-loop or browser mediation.
name: opensearch-project/OpenSearch
description: 🔎 Open source distributed and RESTful search engine.

skillability_rationale: SeleniumBase provides a high-level, task-oriented API for browser automation (e.g., visit(), click(), assert_text(), wait_for_element()) with built-in anti-detection, CDP/Playwright support, and test orchestration. It’s clearly scoped to web interaction tasks, well-documented, and composable via pytest or direct scripting. Deployment requires Python, WebDriver binaries, and optional browsers — moderate friction. Operational risk is moderate due to browser versioning and site anti-bot changes, but mitigated by stealth features. Not a primitive tool (it wraps Selenium/Playwright) nor a platform — it’s a cohesive, reusable workflow skill.
name: seleniumbase/SeleniumBase
description: SeleniumBase simplifies browser automation, scraping, and testing. CDP Mode adds stealth (and works with Playwright). Lots of examples and YouTube tutorials.

skillability_rationale: The library provides a well-defined, atomic capability—embedding and extracting blind/invisible watermarks in images—via simple CLI and Python APIs. It has no external dependencies beyond standard image processing libs, runs deterministically locally, requires no human input or network calls, and delivers high automation value for copyright protection or provenance tracking. Low operational risk: no side effects, no state, no network I/O.
name: guofei9987/blind_watermark
description: Blind&Invisible Watermark ，图片盲水印，提取水印无须原图！

skillability_rationale: clipboard.js provides a well-scoped, deterministic capability—copying/cutting text to the system clipboard via DOM interactions. It has a clear declarative (data attributes) and imperative (JS API) interface, minimal dependencies, and zero runtime risk beyond browser permission constraints. While it’s a low-level primitive (not a full workflow), it’s highly composable into agent actions like 'copy link', 'copy code snippet', or 'extract and copy value'. Its skillability is slightly limited by being browser-context-bound and requiring DOM access—so not directly usable in headless or non-browser agent runtimes without adaptation.
name: zenorocha/clipboard.js
description: :scissors: Modern copy to clipboard. No Flash. Just 3kb gzipped :clipboard:

skillability_rationale: Spotipy is a well-documented, stable wrapper around the Spotify Web API that exposes discrete, task-oriented operations (e.g., search tracks, get user playlists, fetch album info). It has clear inputs/outputs, supports both app- and user-authenticated flows, and composes cleanly into agent workflows. Deployment friction arises from OAuth setup and credential management, but once configured, it’s deterministic and low-risk. Not a primitive tool (it’s not a raw HTTP client) nor a full workflow — it’s a canonical service wrapper.
name: spotipy-dev/spotipy
description: A light weight Python library for the Spotify Web API

skillability_rationale: TruffleHog provides a well-defined, task-bound capability—scanning for, classifying, validating, and analyzing leaked credentials—across diverse sources (Git, filesystems, etc.). It exposes CLI and programmatic Go APIs, enabling integration into automated workflows (e.g., pre-commit hooks, CI/CD). While it’s not a primitive tool (it bundles detection, verification, and analysis logic) nor a full platform, its focused scope, deterministic local execution, and strong automation value make it highly skillifiable. Deployment friction is moderate due to binary dependencies and detector updates; operational risk is elevated (false positives/negatives, API validation side effects), but mitigable via configuration—hence score 4, not 5.
name: trufflesecurity/trufflehog
description: Find, verify, and analyze leaked credentials

skillability_rationale: Promptfoo provides a well-defined, task-bound capability—evaluating and red-teaming LLM prompts, models, and RAG systems—via declarative config and CLI. It wraps diverse LLM providers and evaluation logic into a reusable, automatable unit with clear inputs (prompts, test cases, metrics) and outputs (scores, vulnerabilities, comparisons). Integrates cleanly into CI/CD, supports programmatic use via its library, and has low operational risk (no persistent state or external dependencies beyond API keys). Its purpose is inherently skill-like: 'test and secure AI behavior' is a discrete, high-value agent task.
name: promptfoo/promptfoo
description: Test your prompts, agents, and RAGs. Red teaming/pentesting/vulnerability scanning for AI. Compare performance of GPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, and more. Simple declarative configs with command line and CI/CD integration.

skillability_rationale: rest-client is a well-defined, lightweight HTTP client library that exposes clear, atomic operations (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE) with consistent Ruby DSL syntax. It’s highly composable and deterministic when used in local Ruby environments. Deployment friction arises because it requires Ruby runtime and dependency management (e.g., bundler), limiting zero-config portability. Low operational risk: no external services or stateful dependencies — just HTTP I/O. It qualifies as skillifiable because it wraps network interaction into a reusable, agent-callable unit with predictable inputs/outputs (URL, method, headers, body, response), but remains a primitive (not a full workflow or service wrapper).
name: rest-client/rest-client
description: Simple HTTP and REST client for Ruby, inspired by microframework syntax for specifying actions.

skillability_rationale: Streamlink provides a well-defined, CLI-driven capability to fetch and pipe live or on-demand video streams from dozens of services (e.g., Twitch, YouTube) into players or files. Its task boundary is clear (stream extraction + delivery), it exposes a stable CLI interface with predictable args/outputs, and can be wrapped as a callable agent skill (e.g., 'fetch_stream <url> <quality>'). Deployment requires Python and optional player dependencies (e.g., VLC), adding mild friction. Composability is strong via CLI piping and scripting, though not natively API-first — the Python library exists but is secondary to CLI usage. Operational risk is moderate due to reliance on third-party site structures (plugins may break), but the project is mature and actively maintained.
name: streamlink/streamlink
description: Streamlink is a CLI utility which pipes video streams from various services into a video player

skillability_rationale: E2B provides a well-defined, agent-callable capability: securely executing arbitrary code in isolated cloud sandboxes. Its SDKs expose clean, task-oriented interfaces (create/run/destroy sandbox), integrate seamlessly with LLM agents, and abstract infrastructure complexity. It has clear boundaries, high automation value for code-interpreting agents, minimal ambiguity in intent, and production-grade reliability—making it highly skillifiable as a reusable, remote-execution primitive.
name: e2b-dev/E2B
description: Open-source, secure environment with real-world tools for enterprise-grade agents.

skillability_rationale: YAPF provides a well-defined, deterministic, and idempotent code formatting capability for Python source files. It accepts input (file paths or stdin), applies configurable style rules, and outputs reformatted code or diffs — making it highly suitable as an agent-callable primitive tool. Installation is straightforward via pip, CLI interface is stable and well-documented, and integration into automated workflows (e.g., pre-commit hooks, CI/CD) is common and low-risk. No external dependencies or human judgment required during execution.
name: google/yapf
description: A formatter for Python files

skillability_rationale: LibreTranslate exposes a well-defined, RESTful translation API with clear input (source/target language, text) and output (translated text). It is self-contained, widely adopted, and designed for integration as a reusable service. While self-hosting adds minor deployment friction, the API interface is stable, documented, and composable into multilingual workflows—making it highly skillifiable as an agent-callable translation capability.
name: LibreTranslate/LibreTranslate
description: Free and Open Source Machine Translation API. Self-hosted, offline capable and easy to setup.

skillability_rationale: quicktype is a well-defined, deterministic code-generation tool with clear input (JSON/Schema/GraphQL) and output (typed models/serializers). It has a CLI and programmatic API, integrates into dev workflows, requires no human intervention, carries minimal runtime risk (no side effects), and offers high automation value for type-safe JSON interop. Its narrow, repeatable task boundary makes it highly skillifiable as a primitive agent tool.
name: glideapps/quicktype
description: Generate types and converters from JSON, Schema, and GraphQL

skillability_rationale: This repository provides a well-defined, narrowly scoped MCP server that bridges Figma's design data to AI coding agents (e.g., Cursor) via standardized protocol. It wraps the Figma API into a task-oriented interface—fetching and simplifying layout/styling context for code generation—making it highly skillifiable: clear input (Figma URL), deterministic output (structured design context), minimal side effects, and direct automation value in AI-assisted UI implementation. Requires only a Figma token and MCP client config—no custom logic or human intervention needed during use.
name: GLips/Figma-Context-MCP
description: MCP server to provide Figma layout information to AI coding agents like Cursor

skillability_rationale: gojq is a highly focused, CLI-based JSON query tool with a stable, well-documented interface mirroring jq's expressive syntax. It operates deterministically on stdin/stdout, integrates cleanly into pipelines, and exposes precise data extraction/transformation as a reusable atomic operation—ideal for agent invocation via subprocess or embedded library use. Low operational risk due to pure-Go implementation, no external dependencies, and robust error handling.
name: itchyny/gojq
description: Pure Go implementation of jq

skillability_rationale: DocETL is explicitly designed as a skillifiable, agent-callable system for document-centric ETL: it defines declarative pipeline configurations (YAML), supports LLM-augmented operations (extract, resolve, join, etc.), integrates with local/remote LLMs, and provides both UI playground and programmatic Python API. Its workflow-oriented abstraction, clear input/output contracts per step, and production-ready CLI/Python interface make it highly suitable as a reusable, composable agent skill for unstructured data processing tasks.
name: ucbepic/docetl
description: A system for agentic LLM-powered data processing and ETL

skillability_rationale: Python Fire is a well-defined, lightweight library that transforms Python objects into CLI interfaces — a clear, bounded task with deterministic local execution. It’s highly composable (can wrap any Python object), trivial to deploy (pip install), and low-risk (no external dependencies or side effects). However, as a primitive tool rather than a self-contained skill, it requires developer integration (e.g., wrapping a function/class) and doesn’t expose a standalone API or remote interface — limiting its plug-and-play skillability in agent systems without custom scaffolding.
name: google/python-fire
description: Python Fire is a library for automatically generating command line interfaces (CLIs) from absolutely any Python object.

skillability_rationale: Ragas provides a well-defined, task-oriented service for evaluating LLM applications (e.g., RAG systems) via objective metrics and test generation. Its Python API is clean, documented, and composable with LangChain/LlamaIndex — making it highly suitable as an agent-callable evaluation skill. Minor deployment friction arises from optional LLM dependencies (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic) and embedding model setup, but core functionality works locally and deterministically. Low operational risk: evaluations are read-only, non-destructive, and idempotent.
name: vibrantlabsai/ragas
description: Supercharge Your LLM Application Evaluations 🚀

skillability_rationale: nerdctl provides a well-defined, Docker-compatible CLI interface for containerd operations (run, build, compose, load, ps, etc.), with clear task boundaries and deterministic local execution. It wraps low-level containerd functionality into reusable, scriptable commands—ideal for agent-driven infrastructure automation. While deployment requires containerd and optional dependencies (e.g., rootless helpers, IPFS), the core CLI works out-of-the-box on supported systems and integrates cleanly into CI/CD or orchestration workflows.
name: containerd/nerdctl
description: contaiNERD CTL - Docker-compatible CLI for containerd, with support for Compose, Rootless, eStargz, OCIcrypt, IPFS, ...

skillability_rationale: CryptoJS provides well-defined, atomic cryptographic operations (e.g., AES encrypt/decrypt, SHA256, HMAC) — highly task-clear and composable as primitive building blocks. However, it is officially discontinued and superseded by native Web Crypto API and Node.js crypto module; using it introduces maintenance and security risk. While easy to call locally and deterministic, its declining relevance and lack of updates reduce practical skillability for new agent systems — better served by wrapping native crypto APIs instead.
name: brix/crypto-js
description: JavaScript library of crypto standards.

skillability_rationale: The SDK provides well-documented, modular wrappers for Slack's core APIs (Web API, Socket Mode, OAuth, Webhooks), enabling precise, agent-callable actions like sending messages, reacting to events, or managing auth flows. It’s not a primitive tool (has abstraction) nor a full workflow skill (lacks built-in orchestration), but a high-quality service wrapper. Deployment requires Slack app configuration (OAuth setup, permissions, tokens), adding moderate friction. Operational risk is moderate due to dependency on Slack’s API stability and auth lifecycle management, but mitigated by strong SDK error handling and official support. High task/interface clarity and composability make it highly skillifiable for automation tasks in collaborative contexts.
name: slackapi/node-slack-sdk
description: Slack Developer Kit for Node.js

skillability_rationale: k3sup provides a well-defined, CLI-driven workflow for bootstrapping and managing lightweight Kubernetes (k3s) clusters over SSH — a concrete infrastructure provisioning task. It has clear inputs (SSH host, user, k3s flags) and outputs (KUBECONFIG). While it's highly automatable and composable via scripts or CI, deployment requires pre-existing SSH access and target VM readiness, adding moderate friction. Operational risk is elevated due to cluster mutation (install/uninstall), but mitigated by idempotent design and clear scope. Not a primitive tool (it orchestrates multiple steps), nor a platform adapter (no vendor API abstraction), making 'workflow_skill' the best fit.
name: alexellis/k3sup
description: bootstrap K3s over SSH in < 60s 🚀

skillability_rationale: Conan provides a well-defined, CLI-driven capability for managing C/C++ dependencies—resolving, building, packaging, and deploying binaries across platforms. Its interface is stable, documented, and scriptable, making it highly automatable as an agent skill. While it requires Python and system-level toolchain setup (moderate deployment friction), it wraps complex devops tasks into deterministic, composable commands (e.g., 'conan install', 'conan create'). It is not a primitive tool (has rich logic) nor a full platform, but a mature service wrapper. Operational risk is moderate due to build environment sensitivity and binary compatibility nuances, but mitigated by Conan's stability guarantees and widespread production use.
name: conan-io/conan
description: Conan - The open-source C and C++ package manager

skillability_rationale: JSONata is a well-defined, self-contained library for querying and transforming JSON data. It has a clear input (JSON + expression), deterministic output, minimal dependencies, and a simple API (evaluate()). It can be easily wrapped as an agent skill for JSON extraction, filtering, aggregation, or projection tasks — e.g., 'extract all email addresses from this nested JSON' or 'sum the values of field X'. Low operational risk due to pure functional behavior and no side effects.
name: jsonata-js/jsonata
description: JSONata query and transformation language - http://jsonata.org

skillability_rationale: VisionCamera provides a well-defined, high-value multimedia capability (real-time camera capture, QR/barcode scanning, frame processing) with clear React Native APIs and JSI/Worklet support. It wraps native iOS/Android camera pipelines into a deterministic local execution model. Composability is strong via frame processors and declarative UI, but deployment requires native linking (pod install, Gradle config), raising friction slightly. Operational risk is moderate due to hardware dependency and permission handling, but documentation and community adoption (9k+ stars) mitigate this. Not a primitive tool nor full platform — it's a focused, production-ready service wrapper for camera-centric tasks.
name: mrousavy/react-native-vision-camera
description: 📸 A powerful, high-performance React Native Camera library.

skillability_rationale: The tool exposes a well-defined, single-purpose task—generating stylized city map posters—from clear CLI inputs (city, country, optional theme/coordinates). It wraps OSMnx and Matplotlib into a deterministic, locally executable service with no external API dependencies or stateful services. Outputs are static image files, making it highly automatable, safe, and composable into design or marketing workflows. Minor deployment friction arises from Python environment setup, but uv support mitigates this significantly.
name: originalankur/maptoposter
description: Transform your favorite cities into beautiful, minimalist designs. MapToPoster lets you create and export visually striking map posters with code.

skillability_rationale: This library provides a well-documented, production-ready wrapper around the Google Sheets API with clear task boundaries (read/write rows/cells, manage sheets/docs, export), strong TypeScript support, multiple auth options, and built-in resilience (retries, backoff). It exposes discrete, composable operations that map directly to user-facing productivity tasks (e.g., 'update sales report', 'fetch lead data'), requires no browser automation or human intervention, and integrates cleanly into agent workflows via standard Node.js async calls. Operational risk is moderate due to dependency on Google’s API stability and auth setup complexity, but this is typical and well-mitigated in practice.
name: theoephraim/node-google-spreadsheet
description: Google Sheets API wrapper for Javascript / Typescript

skillability_rationale: Cosmiconfig provides a well-defined, deterministic, and widely adopted capability: locating and loading configuration from standard JS/TS ecosystem locations. It has a clear input (project root + optional config name/options) and output (parsed config object or null). It’s lightweight, synchronous/asynchronous dual API, zero external dependencies, and trivial to wrap as an agent-callable function—e.g., 'loadConfig(toolName, cwd?)'. Low operational risk: no side effects, no network I/O, no mutable state by default.
name: cosmiconfig/cosmiconfig
description: Find and load configuration from a package.json property, rc file, TypeScript module, and more!

skillability_rationale: Multipass provides a well-defined, CLI-driven interface to manage lightweight Ubuntu VMs across platforms—ideal for automating dev/test infrastructure setup. It wraps low-level hypervisors (KVM/Hyper-V/QEMU/VirtualBox) into a consistent task-level abstraction (e.g., 'launch', 'exec', 'shell', 'stop'). While installation requires platform-specific steps (e.g., enabling Hyper-V on Windows, disabling macOS firewall), the CLI is stable, predictable, and scriptable. It lacks native API or remote orchestration hooks, limiting it to local deterministic execution—but that aligns well with agent-executed infrastructure provisioning tasks in CI/dev workflows.
name: canonical/multipass
description: Multipass orchestrates virtual Ubuntu instances

skillability_rationale: Gotenberg exposes a well-defined, RESTful, multipart API for converting diverse document formats (HTML, DOCX, Excel, etc.) to PDF — a discrete, high-value automation task. It’s containerized, production-ready, and requires no client-side logic; agents can call it via HTTP with clear input/output contracts. Minimal configuration, strong documentation, and broad format support make it highly skillifiable as a reusable document conversion capability.
name: gotenberg/gotenberg
description: A developer-friendly API for converting many document formats into PDF files, and more!

skillability_rationale: SmsForwarder wraps Android system events (SMS, calls, notifications) and forwards them to diverse external services (DingTalk, WeCom, Feishu, Telegram, email, webhook, etc.) via well-defined configuration — making it a high-value, reusable service wrapper. Its task boundary is clear (event-triggered forwarding), interface is declarative (rule-based config + REST-like control API), and it composes well into automation workflows (e.g., alerting, remote monitoring). Deployment requires Android installation and permissions (moderate friction), and operational risk stems from Android OS permission changes and background execution limitations — but not from inherent instability or data handling flaws. It is not a platform or workflow itself, nor does it require human-in-the-loop for core operation, just initial setup.
name: pppscn/SmsForwarder
description: 短信转发器——监控Android手机短信、来电、APP通知，并根据指定规则转发到其他手机：钉钉群自定义机器人、钉钉企业内机器人、企业微信群机器人、飞书机器人、企业微信应用消息、邮箱、bark、webhook、Telegram机器人、Server酱、PushPlus、手机短信等。包括主动控制服务端与客户端，让你轻松远程发短信、查短信、查通话、查话簿、查电量等。（V3.0 新增）PS.这个APK主要是学习与自用，如有BUG请提ISSUE，同时欢迎大家提PR指正

skillability_rationale: The library provides a well-defined, task-bound capability—semantic segmentation of images—via a clean, high-level PyTorch API. It is deterministic, locally executable, and highly automatable for image analysis pipelines. Composability is strong (integrates with training loops, metrics, ONNX export), but deployment requires Python/PyTorch environment setup and GPU considerations, slightly increasing friction. Low operational risk: no external APIs, no stateful services, MIT-licensed, widely tested. It’s not a full workflow or service wrapper—it’s a reusable primitive tool for pixel-level image understanding.
name: qubvel-org/segmentation_models.pytorch
description: Semantic segmentation models with 500+ pretrained convolutional and transformer-based backbones.

skillability_rationale: Doctrine Inflector provides a well-defined, deterministic string transformation capability (e.g., pluralize/singularize/camelCase/underscore) with a simple, stable API. It’s lightweight, dependency-free, and embeddable — ideal as a reusable primitive in code-generation or naming-convention automation tasks. While highly skillifiable, its narrow scope limits standalone high-impact automation value compared to broader tools.
name: doctrine/inflector
description: Doctrine Inflector is a small library that can perform string manipulations with regard to uppercase/lowercase and singular/plural forms of words.

skillability_rationale: FreeAskInternet implements a well-defined, end-to-end workflow: accept user question → perform multi-engine web search (via local SearxNG) → fetch and summarize result content → route to a free LLM API (e.g., Kimi/Qwen) or local LLM (Ollama/llama.cpp) → generate and stream an answer. It is self-contained, locally executable, and exposes a clear input (question string) and output (answer text + sources). Composability is slightly limited by tight coupling to its own UI and search pipeline, but the core logic is modular enough to be wrapped as an agent-callable function. Operational risk is moderate due to reliance on unofficial/free LLM APIs (potential instability), but local execution and no GPU requirement enhance reliability and privacy.
name: nashsu/FreeAskInternet
description: FreeAskInternet is a completely free, PRIVATE and LOCALLY running search aggregator & answer generate using MULTI LLMs, without GPU needed. The user can ask a question and the system will  make a multi engine search and combine the search result to LLM and generate the answer based on search results. It's all FREE to use.

skillability_rationale: patch-package provides a well-defined, atomic DevOps task—creating and applying patches to npm/yarn dependencies—via simple CLI commands. It has clear inputs (package name, modified files) and deterministic outputs (.patch files + patched node_modules). It’s composable in CI/CD pipelines and postinstall hooks, though requires careful handling of patch file placement and caching. Deployment friction arises from environment-specific setup (e.g., Heroku env vars, Docker copy order), but tooling is mature and widely adopted. Low operational risk: patches are scoped, reversible, and applied idempotently. It’s skillifiable as a reusable agent action for dependency remediation workflows.
name: ds300/patch-package
description: Fix broken node modules instantly  🏃🏽‍♀️💨

skillability_rationale: GIXY is a well-defined, CLI-based static analyzer for Nginx configs with clear input (config file path) and structured output (security issue reports). It performs a single, high-value DevOps task—detecting misconfigurations—with deterministic local execution, low operational risk (read-only analysis), and no side effects. Its interface is simple (CLI + optional JSON output), easily composable into CI/CD or agent workflows, and requires minimal setup (pip install).
name: yandex/gixy
description: Nginx configuration static analyzer

skillability_rationale: The action provides a well-scoped, deterministic capability—filtering CI/CD steps based on file changes—with clear inputs (paths, base branch) and outputs (boolean flags like 'changed'). It wraps GitHub's REST API and git CLI in a reusable, declarative interface, integrates natively into GitHub Actions workflows, and enables high-value conditional automation (e.g., skipping tests in unchanged modules). Low operational risk: it’s read-only, stateless, and widely adopted (3k+ stars, used by Sentry, web.dev). Its service-wrapper granularity and remote-api-mediated execution make it ideal as an agent-callable skill for devops-aware agents.
name: dorny/paths-filter
description: Conditionally run actions based on files modified by PR, feature branch or pushed commits

skillability_rationale: The repository provides a well-defined, atomic task—verifying email existence without sending mail—exposed via a clean HTTP API and CLI. It’s easily integrable as a service wrapper in agent workflows (e.g., pre-signup validation). Deployment requires Docker/port 25 access (moderate friction), and operational risk stems from SMTP dependency and potential false negatives/positives, but it’s deterministic and low-side-effect. Not a primitive tool (has logic beyond simple regex) nor a full workflow—just a focused, reusable verification capability.
name: reacherhq/check-if-email-exists
description: Check if an email address exists without sending any email, written in Rust. Comes with a ⚙️ HTTP backend.

skillability_rationale: Datasette wraps SQLite databases into a self-contained, queryable web service with RESTful JSON APIs and a browser UI. Its core task — serving ad-hoc SQL queries over structured data — is well-defined, deterministic, and highly automatable via HTTP. It can be invoked as a local subprocess or containerized service, exposing clean programmatic interfaces (e.g., /db/table.json?sql=...). While deployment requires installing Python/dependencies or Docker, it’s lightweight and sandboxed; failure modes are low-risk (no side effects, read-only by default). Composability is strong for data exploration pipelines but slightly limited by its focus on SQLite and lack of native async orchestration hooks.
name: simonw/datasette
description: An open source multi-tool for exploring and publishing data

skillability_rationale: The library is a well-documented, officially maintained wrapper for the OpenAI API, exposing clear, task-oriented methods (e.g., chat completions, responses, file uploads, streaming). It has strong interface clarity and composability—ideal for wrapping as an agent skill that delegates LLM calls. Deployment friction is moderate due to dependency on API keys and network availability. Operational risk is medium (rate limits, cost, model deprecations), but mitigated by stability of OpenAI’s platform. Not a primitive tool (it’s not low-level) nor a workflow skill (no orchestration logic), so 'service_wrapper' is most accurate.
name: openai/openai-node
description: Official JavaScript / TypeScript library for the OpenAI API

skillability_rationale: Shimmy provides a clear, well-defined capability: serving local LLM inference via OpenAI-compatible REST endpoints. It wraps GGUF/SafeTensors model loading and inference into a deterministic, single-binary service with zero Python dependencies. Its interface is fully standardized (OpenAI API), making it trivial to integrate into agent toolchains. It’s highly composable — any agent that supports OpenAI-style chat/completion calls can use it immediately. Deployment friction is moderate only due to model management (downloading, placing GGUF files), not the server itself. Operational risk is low: no external dependencies, no telemetry, MIT licensed, audited, and designed for local, private execution. This is a textbook skillifiable service wrapper.
name: Michael-A-Kuykendall/shimmy
description: ⚡ Python-free Rust inference server — OpenAI-API compatible. GGUF + SafeTensors, hot model swap, auto-discovery, single binary. FREE now, FREE forever.

skillability_rationale: auto-sklearn provides a well-defined, scikit-learn–compatible interface for automating ML model selection and hyperparameter tuning — a high-value, repeatable research/analysis task. It wraps complex AutoML logic (Bayesian optimization, meta-learning, SMAC) into a simple fit/predict API, making it highly skillifiable as a service wrapper. Deployment friction arises from dependencies (e.g., SWIG, compilers) and resource demands (CPU/memory/time), slightly lowering score. Operational risk is moderate due to non-determinism in search and potential for overfitting or long runtimes, but mitigated by timeouts and robust defaults.
name: automl/auto-sklearn
description: Automated Machine Learning with scikit-learn

skillability_rationale: httpbin is a well-defined, stateless HTTP testing service with a stable, documented REST API. It exposes atomic endpoints (e.g., /get, /post, /status/200) ideal for wrapping as reusable agent skills to simulate, validate, or debug HTTP interactions. No authentication, minimal dependencies, and wide adoption make it highly skillifiable — especially for testing, integration scaffolding, or teaching network behavior.
name: postmanlabs/httpbin
description: HTTP Request & Response Service, written in Python + Flask.

skillability_rationale: cross is a well-defined, CLI-driven tool that wraps Docker/Podman to enable deterministic cross-compilation and cross-testing of Rust crates — a clear, repeatable devops task. It integrates seamlessly into CI/CD and agent workflows via command-line invocation, has stable semantics, and exposes no ambiguous or stateful behavior. Deployment friction arises from requiring container runtime setup (Docker/Podman + binfmt_misc on Linux), slightly lowering accessibility. Not a primitive tool (it orchestrates containers) nor a full workflow skill (no multi-step logic beyond cargo-like commands), so 'service_wrapper' fits best.
name: cross-rs/cross
description: “Zero setup” cross compilation and “cross testing” of Rust crates

skillability_rationale: TanStack Query is a powerful, well-documented library for managing server-state in frontend frameworks, but it is not inherently skillifiable as a standalone agent callable unit: it requires integration into a UI framework (e.g., React), relies on user-provided fetchers/mutators, and lacks a self-contained, task-oriented API surface. While it wraps data fetching/caching logic, it's designed as a developer-facing SDK—not an atomic, parameterized, agent-invocable skill. Its high composability and clarity make it *foundationally* valuable, but low task boundary definition and no built-in remote or declarative execution interface limit direct skillability.
name: TanStack/query
description: 🤖 Powerful asynchronous state management, server-state utilities and data fetching for the web. TS/JS, React Query, Solid Query, Svelte Query and Vue Query.
